skill.name: tools-ui
skill.description: Tool lifecycle UI components for React and Next.js. Display agent tool calls with pending, progress, approval, and results states. Provides human-in-the-loop approval flows and tool output visualization.
skill.summary: React components for displaying AI agent tool calls with approval flows and lifecycle states.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 40 patterns that are all false positives. External command detections are Markdown code blocks (bash, tsx) in documentation, not executable shell code. Network URL detections are legitimate documentation references to ui.inference.sh component library. Cryptographic warnings match plain text in descriptions, not actual crypto implementations. This skill is documentation-only for React UI components with no security risks.

content.user_title: Display AI Agent Tool Calls with Approval Flows
content.value_statement: Building AI agents that call tools requires clear UI feedback for users. These React components provide ready-made interfaces for showing tool lifecycle states from pending through results.
actual_capabilities: Display tool calls in pending state while awaiting execution
Show running progress indicators for active tool calls
Render approval dialogs for tools requiring human confirmation
Present tool results with success or error status styling
Auto-assign icons based on tool name patterns
Integrate with shadcn component registry via CLI
limitations: Requires React 18 and Next.js 13 or later
Components are UI-only and do not execute tools themselves
Requires integration with a backend agent runtime for tool execution
Icon mapping uses predefined patterns and may not match custom tool names

use_cases.title: Agent Tool Display
use_cases.description: Show users what tools their AI agent is calling in real-time with status indicators and results visualization.

use_cases.title: Approval Workflow Interface
use_cases.description: Implement human-in-the-loop flows where users must approve sensitive tool calls like sending emails or deleting files before execution.

use_cases.title: MCP Tool Monitoring
use_cases.description: Display Model Context Protocol tool invocations with progress states for debugging and transparency in AI applications.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Tool Call Display
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a React component that shows when Claude Code is calling a tool, displaying the tool name and arguments in a styled card with a loading spinner.

prompt_templates.title: Create Approval Dialog
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an approval dialog component for dangerous tool calls that shows the tool name, arguments, and has Approve and Deny buttons with appropriate styling.

prompt_templates.title: Build Tool Results View
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a component that displays tool execution results with color-coded status indicators for success and error states, including collapsible result details.

prompt_templates.title: Full Tool Lifecycle Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete tool lifecycle display component that automatically renders the appropriate UI based on tool status: pending, running, approval required, success with results, or error with message.

output_examples.input: Show a tool call for reading a file
output_examples.output: A card displays 'read_file' with a file icon, shows the path argument '/src/index.ts', and includes a blue progress spinner indicating the tool is running.

output_examples.input: Request approval to send an email
output_examples.output: An approval dialog shows 'send_email' with a mail icon, displays the recipient and subject, and presents green Approve and red Deny buttons for user confirmation.

best_practices: Always show tool arguments to users for transparency before and during execution
Require explicit approval for tools that modify data or send external communications
Use consistent color coding: blue for running, green for success, red for errors, yellow for approval required
anti_patterns: Hiding tool execution from users reduces trust and transparency in AI agent behavior
Skipping approval steps for sensitive operations can lead to unintended consequences
Showing raw tool output without formatting can expose sensitive data or overwhelm users

faq.question: What AI platforms do these components support?
faq.answer: The components work with any AI agent that calls tools, including Claude Code, Codex, and custom agents using the Model Context Protocol.

faq.question: Do I need a backend to use these components?
faq.answer: Yes, these are UI components only. You need a backend agent runtime to actually execute the tools. The components display the lifecycle states passed from your agent.

faq.question: How do I install these components?
faq.answer: Use the shadcn CLI: npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.inference.sh/r/tools.json to add all tool UI components to your project.

faq.question: Can I customize the appearance of the tool cards?
faq.answer: Yes, all components accept standard className props for custom styling and use Tailwind CSS classes by default.

faq.question: How does the approval flow work?
faq.answer: When a tool has requiresApproval set to true, render the ToolApproval component with onApprove and onDeny callbacks. Call these callbacks based on user interaction to continue or cancel the tool execution.

faq.question: What happens if a tool name does not match any icon pattern?
faq.answer: Tools with unrecognized names display a default wrench icon. You can extend the icon mapping logic to handle custom tool name patterns.
